mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero, persistent near-zero policy rates continuously suppress the equity discount rate, mechanically supporting rising S&P 500 valuations over rolling 20-day windows regardless of short-term news flow. This is an always-on carry/valuation tailwind during the zero-lower-bound period.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19 percent as of Nov 2010, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level against forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during the ZIRP period, or forward returns are not significantly positive relative to non-ZIRP baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 9.5 percent signals labor market slack severe enough to trigger expectations of continued extraordinary monetary accommodation (e.g., QE2 extension), which supports risk asset prices over a quarterly horizon. The signal activates only once UNRATE crosses this high threshold, distinguishing crisis-level slack from normal-cycle softness.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8 percent as of Nov 2010
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9.5 percent, forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate falls below 50 percent across the trigger sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in payroll employment growth (PAYEMS month-over-month) and industrial production growth (INDPRO month-over-month) jointly confirm a broadening real-economy expansion that is not visible from either series alone; this joint confirmation reduces recession-tail risk priced into equities and drives positive forward returns over one month. Neither series alone reliably signals this regime shift due to noise and revision risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0.5 percent simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 130539 thousand and INDPRO 93.90 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with confirming labor and production data
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO show joint acceleration, forward 20-day returns underperform the case where only one series accelerates, or joint-signal Rank IC is not higher than either single-series IC by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts (HOUST) from post-crisis lows signals renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, which feeds through construction employment and durable goods demand to support equity prices over a quarterly horizon. This mechanism is specific to the early post-recession recovery regime when housing is the marginal swing factor in GDP, and is expected to be much weaker in mature expansions where housing is a smaller growth driver.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 519 thousand units as of Oct 2010, near historic lows
failure_condition: HOUST level or 3-month change shows no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with forward 60-day SPX returns during the post-2009 recovery window, or the relationship reverses sign in a later mature-expansion sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) reflect real-time strengthening in manufacturing output that precedes analyst earnings upgrades, producing modest positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following trading week. This is a continuous, always-on momentum linkage between production data releases and near-term equity direction.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.8961 as of Nov 2010, trending up from 2009 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in headline CPI above a 3 percent year-over-year pace, while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, raises fears that the Federal Reserve will be forced to reverse extraordinary accommodation sooner than markets expect, compressing equity valuations over the following month. The signal only activates once inflation crosses this threshold given the current ultra-low rate backdrop.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3 percent while FEDFUNDS < 0.5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.146 as of Nov 2010, FEDFUNDS at 0.19 percent
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: Instances where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3 percent under near-zero FEDFUNDS produce non-negative average forward 20-day SPX returns, or hit rate for negative direction is below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a stagnant or worsening unemployment rate (UNRATE) creates a divergence signal: strong consumer spending despite weak labor markets suggests spending is unsustainable (drawing on savings or credit), which raises short-term downside risk for equities within one trading week as markets reassess consumption durability. Neither series alone captures this fragility signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5 percent while UNRATE month-over-month change >= 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378706 million and UNRATE at 9.8 percent as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: high unemployment recovery with consumption-labor divergence
failure_condition: When the RSAFS-strong/UNRATE-weak divergence condition triggers, forward 5-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or hit rate for negative direction is below 50 percent across triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound monetary regime combined with subdued real GDP growth, continued balance-sheet expansion signaled indirectly through persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS alongside below-trend GDPC1 growth reflects an ongoing quantitative easing environment that supports risk asset valuations over a quarterly horizon; this mechanism is regime-specific and would reverse once FEDFUNDS begins normalizing or GDP growth returns to trend.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19 percent and GDPC1 at 13277.4 billion as of latest snapshot, indicating below-trend recovery
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary policy with below-trend GDP growth
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns during periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS and below-trend GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the relationship inverts once FEDFUNDS begins rising while GDP remains below trend.
